  <entry>
    <id>http://arxiv.org/abs/quant-ph/0201082v1</id>
    <title>Quantum Computers and Quantum Computer Languages: Quantum Assembly Language and Quantum C Language</title>
    <updated>2002-01-18T15:08:05Z</updated>
    <link href="https://arxiv.org/abs/quant-ph/0201082v1" rel="alternate" type="text/html"/>
    <link href="https://arxiv.org/pdf/quant-ph/0201082v1" rel="related" type="application/pdf" title="pdf"/>
    <summary>  We show a representation of Quantum Computers defines Quantum Turing Machines with associated Quantum Grammars. We then create examples of Quantum Grammars. Lastly we develop an algebraic approach to high level Quantum Languages using Quantum Assembly language and Quantum C language as examples.</summary>
    <category term="quant-ph" scheme="http://arxiv.org/schemas/atom"/>
    <category term="cs.PL" scheme="http://arxiv.org/schemas/atom"/>
    <published>2002-01-18T15:08:05Z</published>
    <arxiv:comment>32 pages</arxiv:comment>
    <arxiv:primary_category term="quant-ph"/>
    <author>
      <name>Stephen Blaha</name>
    </author>
  </entry>
